provenance.title: A Noise-Aware Quantum Algorithm for Credit Valuation Adjustments on Real Quantum Hardware
affiliations: BBVA Corporate & Investment Banking, Madrid, Spain
Universidad Autónoma de Madrid, Madrid, Spain
provenance.abstract: Credit Valuation Adjustment (CVA) requires repeated risk-neutral expectation estimation, making it a natural test bed for quantum amplitude estimation. We develop an end-to-end, noise-aware quantum workflow for CVA, covering market calibration, discretisation, oracle construction, hardware execution and error-budget analysis, introducing contrast-aware Bayesian iterative QAE (CABIQAE).
key_claims: Develops an end-to-end noise-aware quantum CVA workflow spanning market calibration, discretisation, oracle construction, hardware execution and error-budget analysis for a correlated two-asset netting set

Introduces contrast-aware Bayesian iterative quantum amplitude estimation (CABIQAE), which folds experimentally calibrated Grover-contrast decay into the Bayesian likelihood and Grover-depth scheduler
Shows CABIQAE matches BAE's hardware-replay accuracy while using shallower amplified circuits and far lower classical post-processing runtime (1.25s vs 45.72s median)

Formally decomposes total CVA pipeline error into finite-grid/truncation, quantum-encoding/training, and amplitude-estimation contributions, showing discretisation and encoding dominate (>65%) while AE residual is secondary (<4%)
Validates the full pipeline on real IBM Quantum hardware (ibm_basquecountry, Heron r2) via a hardware-replay methodology using Q-CTRL Performance Management error mitigation

architecture.primary_variant: Six-qubit two-asset CVA quantum encoding circuit (m=2 time qubits, n=4 asset qubits, 3 ancillas) with CABIQAE amplitude-estimation post-processing loop

modules.name: G_P / G_theta (QCBM state-preparation unitary)

parameters.connectivity: sparse heavy-hex-compatible entangler graph, max degree 3, zero SWAP embedding

modules.notes: Trained via cross-entropy minimisation between Born distribution P_theta and target finite-grid joint probability tensor P_target; statevector-trained circuit selected over noisy-trained circuit (Figure 8).

modules.name: R_v (exposure controlled-rotation ansatz, CRCA)
modules.output_shape: sqrt(1-F_phi(x))|0> + sqrt(F_phi(x))|1> on ancilla a_v, conditioned on full (i,j) register

parameters.topology: alternating outward/inward snake layers over 7-qubit patch (6 register + 1 ancilla), max physical degree 3

modules.notes: Most demanding controlled-rotation block because positive exposure V+_{i,j} is non-linear and non-separable across time and asset coordinates. Trained by MSE loss against target v~_{i,j} = V+_{i,j}/C_v.

modules.name: R_p (discount-factor CRCA)
modules.output_shape: sqrt(1-F_phi_p(i))|0> + sqrt(F_phi_p(i))|1>, conditioned only on time register i
modules.notes: Target function p~_i = D(0,t_i)/C_p; deterministic function of monitoring date only.

modules.name: R_q (default-probability CRCA)
modules.output_shape: sqrt(1-F_phi_q(i))|0> + sqrt(F_phi_q(i))|1>, conditioned only on time register i
modules.notes: Target function q~_i = Delta q(t_i)/C_q; deterministic function of monitoring date only.

modules.name: A_Theta (full CVA encoding unitary)
modules.notes: A = R_q(phi_q*) R_p(phi_p*) R_v(phi_v*) G_theta*. Composition order matters: state prep first, then exposure, discount, default rotations.

modules.name: Pi_111 (marked-subspace projector / readout)
modules.notes: Pi_111 = I ⊗ |111><111|; marked subspace spanned by all |i>|j>|111>, not a single basis state of the full register.

modules.name: Q (Grover iterate / amplitude amplification operator)
modules.input_shape: state A|0> in 2D good/bad invariant subspace
modules.notes: Standard Grover/amplitude-amplification construction (Brassard et al. 2002); K := 2k+1 counts oracle queries per amplified circuit.

modules.name: CABIQAE classical estimator (Bayesian post-processing loop)
modules.input_shape: measurement counts (successes/shots) from Q^{k_t}A circuits across adaptive stages t
modules.output_shape: point estimate a_hat and credible interval [a_l, a_u] for the marked amplitude a_CVA

parameters.prior_family: Beta distribution over observed success probability p_t (not over ideal sin^2(K*theta))

modules.notes: Extends Beta-BIQAE (Li et al. 2026) by replacing the ideal amplified-observation likelihood with a hardware-calibrated contrast-decay model, and by biasing the Grover-depth scheduler (FindNextK) toward posterior-Fisher-informative depths rather than maximal depth.

connections.tensor_name: A used to build Q = -A S0 A^dagger Sf
a_hat_AE rescaled by M(1-R_CVA)C_v C_p C_q

differences.qubits: m=2 time + n=4 asset (2 per underlying) + 3 ancillas = 9 total

differences.purpose: isolated amplitude-estimation-algorithm validation (Section 3.2.2, 4.1); free final rotation angle phi generates multiple target amplitudes for benchmarking BAE/BIQAE/CABIQAE/DCS

mathematical_spec.name: Discretised CVA (finite time-market grid)
mathematical_spec.latex: CVA_\Delta = (1-R_{CVA}) \sum_{i=1}^{M} D(0,t_i)\, \Delta q(t_i) \sum_j Q_D(S_{t_i}\in B_j)\, V^+_{i,j}

mathematical_spec.name: KL divergence diagnostic (QCBM quality metric, not training loss)
Posterior pullback to latent-angle credible interval
Ideal QAE additive error bound (Brassard et al. 2002)
CDS bootstrap par-spread condition (default curve calibration)

tensor_semantics.name: asset registers |j>_S = |j1>_S1 ... |jd>_Sd
tensor_semantics.shape_notation: [n] qubits, n=4 total (n1=n2=2 per underlying, d=2 underlyings, N1=N2=4 price bins each)

training_pipeline.curriculum: Layer-depth (L) sweep performed per block (QCBM L=2..16; CRCA depth comparisons) with statevector-vs-noisy-training comparison (Figure 8) used to select final L per block (Table 5): L=6 for G_theta, L=1 for R_p/R_q, L=2 for R_v

datasets.name: LSEG Workspace market data (EURO STOXX 50 & SMI equity closes/dividends, implied-vol surfaces, EUR OIS curve, Iberdrola CDS par-spread curve)
Synthetic two-asset CVA netting set (1 EURO STOXX 50 call + 1 SMI put, Table 3)

metrics: relative CVA / relative amplitude error (median across R=300 trajectories)
query cost N_q (number of oracle calls to A/Q)
coverage probability (fraction of runs where true amplitude lies in returned credible interval)
maximum Grover amplification factor K reached
KL divergence (KL_epsilon) between target and QCBM-generated distribution
log-log query-scaling exponent beta (fit of log(error) vs log(N_q))

reported_results.metric: CABIQAE median classical runtime, seconds (hardware-replay, validation circuit)
BAE median classical runtime, seconds (hardware-replay, validation circuit)

baselines: BIQAE (Bayesian Iterative Quantum Amplitude Estimation, Li et al. 2026)
DCS (Direct Circuit Sampling, unamplified k=0 baseline)
evaluation_protocol.compute: IBM Quantum ibm_basquecountry (156-qubit Heron r2 superconducting processor, heavy-hex topology), accessed via Basque Quantum (BasQ); Q-CTRL Performance Management Qiskit Function used for hardware error suppression; statevector/noisy simulations via Qiskit Statevector and Aer simulators
special_conditions: Hardware-replay methodology: hardware measures amplified circuit responses once per Grover power on real backend, then R=300 independent adaptive trajectories are statistically replayed from that empirical response rather than each running live on the QPU
Readout error mitigation applied via calibration circuits (r0, r1, affine correction)
Contrast-decay model tau_c fitted per circuit family via supervised (oracle-assisted) calibration using known ideal amplitude, not blind
BAE omitted from final six-qubit CVA comparison due to prohibitive classical runtime over hundreds of trajectories

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Classical optimizer used to train QCBM (G_theta) and CRCA (R_v, R_p, R_q) parameters is not explicitly named in the paper; a standard gradient-based or gradient-free variational optimizer (e.g. Adam, L-BFGS, or COBYLA/SPSA as commonly used for QCBM training per Liu et al. 2018) is assumed.
implementation_assumptions.basis: literature default / common practice for QCBM and variational quantum circuit training

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Exact learning-rate values and per-iteration schedule for each variational block are not given numerically in the main text; approximate iteration counts (e.g. ~1100 for QCBM, ~4000 for R_v) are read off training-curve figures (Figure 8, Figure 9) rather than stated directly.
alternatives: exact hyperparameters may be available in project code repository (Borràs, 2026) not reproduced in the paper text

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Random seeds for quantum statevector/finite-shot simulation and for CABIQAE/BIQAE/BAE trajectory sampling are not stated (only the classical Monte Carlo benchmark seed, 10^5, is given).
alternatives: seeds may be fixed in the released code repository

implementation_assumptions.assumption: The internal transpilation, layout, routing and error-suppression steps performed by Q-CTRL's Performance Management Qiskit Function are proprietary/managed and not disclosed; they are treated as a black-box hardware-execution layer, and ISA depth/2Q-gate counts reported for Q-CTRL-managed circuits are only approximated via a separate hardware-aware proxy transpilation.
implementation_assumptions.basis: explicitly stated by the authors as an internal, non-observable process
alternatives: assume proxy metrics in Table 16 exactly equal internally-managed circuit resources (less likely per the authors' own caveat)

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Software framework is Qiskit (IBM Quantum ecosystem), based on explicit citations to Qiskit Statevector/Aer simulators and IBM Quantum backend documentation.
alternatives: a custom simulation layer built on top of Qiskit primitives

ambiguities.description: The ideal-regime validation trajectories cover 8 distinct target amplitudes (from varying phi), while the hardware-replay regime uses a single hardware-calibrated amplitude (a_true=0.36027). The precise correspondence between the listed phi values and which specific trajectories appear in which panel of Figure 6/13 is not made fully explicit in the main text.
ambiguities.primary_assumption: Single amplitude a_true=0.36027 used throughout hardware-replay; all 8 listed amplitudes pooled together for the ideal-regime aggregate curves in Figure 6 (top) and Table 2
alternatives: each amplitude might be plotted/fit separately in supplementary material not shown

ambiguities.location: Appendix A.2 (Q-CTRL Performance Management) and Table 16
ambiguities.description: Because Q-CTRL's managed workflow does not expose its final compiled circuit, the ISA depth/2Q-gate-count figures reported for the actual hardware-executed CVA circuits cannot be independently verified against Table 16, which is stated to be a proxy/projection rather than the exact executed circuit.
ambiguities.primary_assumption: Table 16 metrics are representative structural proxies; true Q-CTRL-managed circuit resources may differ

ambiguities.location: Section 3.2.3 (layer-depth selection for QCBM/CRCA blocks) and Figure 8
ambiguities.description: Final layer depths (L=6 for G_theta, L=1 for R_p/R_q, L=2 for R_v, Table 5) are justified by a qualitative expressivity/trainability/depth trade-off discussion rather than a fully specified quantitative selection rule or stopping criterion applicable beyond this instance.
ambiguities.primary_assumption: Reported L values in Table 5 are the final fixed choices used for all downstream six-qubit CVA experiments
alternatives: other L values tested during the sweep in Figure 8/9 could plausibly have been chosen under a different selection criterion
